mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment signals ongoing slack in the labor market, depressing consumer spending and corporate earnings. Elevated UNRATE at any level predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns as the economy struggles to recover from the recession.
failure_condition: If UNRATE is above 6% and S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods over a 2-year walk-forward window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below a critical threshold indicate severe contraction in residential investment and household wealth. Such low levels of HOUST suggest that the housing sector is in deep slump, acting as a strong negative signal for forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: After HOUST drops below 700, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over the subsequent 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous near-zero interest rates and depressed retail sales indicate that monetary policy has lost its conventional effectiveness while consumer demand remains crippled. This joint condition amplifies negative recessionary pressure, leading to poor forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1% and RSAFS below 350,000 million dollars
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of cases over a 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: During a deep recession defined by very high unemployment and contracting GDP, further increases in UNRATE predict cumulative negative earnings shocks as the labor market continues to deteriorate, exerting sustained downward pressure on equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 decline in Q4 2008 and Q1 2009
failure_condition: In the defined deep recession regime, the correlation between UNRATE and forward 60-day returns is zero or positive over any 6-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops to extremely low levels (near zero), it signals aggressive monetary easing that compresses discount rates and stimulates economic activity, providing a short-term boost to equity prices within a week.
failure_condition: After the trigger, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over a 12-month walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below a threshold that indicates severe supply-side contraction, as measured by INDPRO, signals ongoing weakness in manufacturing and broader economy. This implies persistent downward pressure on corporate profits and negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: After INDPRO crosses below 102, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high unemployment and declining payrolls indicate a double weakening in the labor market. The combination of elevated UNRATE and falling PAYEMS intensifies fears of prolonged recession, pushing equity prices lower over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE above 7% and PAYEMS month-over-month decline
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the joint signal's rank IC with forward 20-day returns is less than 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity trap where the federal funds rate is zero and inflation is very low, the conventional transmission mechanism of accommodative monetary policy is impaired. Low FEDFUNDS no longer stimulates growth; instead, it signals a dysfunctional economy, leading to negative short-term equity returns as deflation risks dominate.
regime_hint: liquidity trap (FEDFUNDS near zero and CPIAUCSL growth below 1% YoY)
failure_condition: In the liquidity trap regime, the correlation between FEDFUNDS and forward 5-day returns is positive, contradicting the negative direction.
